purpose: Map each Gate 2 audit scope to Philippine source realities before any building-specific evidence search starts.
global_safety_rule: Missing public evidence must produce not_found, manual_request_needed, or not_assessable_from_public_web; it must never be converted into a compliance, safety, or risk conclusion.

primary_source_types: national permit workflow law and joint memoranda
LGU Office or Department of the Building Official pages
LGU citizen charters and permit checklists
LGU online application or status portals
manual LGU certification or records request paths

example_sources.name: DILG-DPWH-DICT-DTI JMC 2018-01
example_sources.access_notes: Public legal/process context for building permit, occupancy permit, FSEC, and FSIC workflow language.

example_sources.name: Quezon City Department of the Building Official
example_sources.access_notes: Public office page for services, contacts, and process orientation; not a general public building-specific permit registry.

example_sources.name: Quezon City DBO Citizen's Charter 2026
example_sources.access_notes: Public PDF describing requirements and workflows for DBO services.

example_sources.name: Davao City Office of the City Building Official
example_sources.access_notes: Public OCBO service page with building permit, occupancy permit, annual inspection, certification, and complaint functions.

example_sources.name: Davao City Online Building Permit System application status
example_sources.access_notes: Public-facing portal path, but useful building-specific status checks require application details.

example_sources.name: Cebu City Office of the Building Official
example_sources.access_notes: Public city office page for OBO context and local routing.

example_sources.name: Pasig OBO Citizen's Charter
example_sources.access_notes: Public charter for local OBO process and request paths.

example_sources.name: PSA construction statistics
example_sources.access_notes: Useful only for aggregate construction context, not proof about a named building.

required_user_inputs: confirmed exact building, branch, complex, or tenant target
city or LGU jurisdiction and full address or barangay
record type requested: building permit, occupancy permit, annual inspection, certification, or complaint
permit, occupancy, application, business permit, or tracking number if available
owner, developer, applicant, lessee, or authorized representative details if the user has them
year range or construction, renovation, occupancy, or inspection window
expected_public_result_shape: public process checklist, office, fees, forms, and contact route
portal status only when the user provides enough application identifiers
manual_request_needed when building-specific records are not publicly searchable
not_found when public web search finds no building-specific record after scoped search
citation-backed caveat that absence of a public result does not mean no permit or occupancy permit exists
limitations: There is no reliable nationwide public property-level search for building permits or occupancy permits.
LGU portals are often applicant-facing workflows rather than public audit databases.
Records may be offline, archived, restricted to authorized parties, or available only through certification requests.
PSA construction statistics are aggregate data and cannot prove anything about a named building.
LGU naming, office structure, and online maturity vary widely by city or municipality.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim the place has no permit because no public web result was found.
Do not claim the place is code-compliant from a permit process page.
Do not claim the place is safe, unsafe, or fit for occupancy without a current official record that directly says so.
Do not treat a business listing, map listing, or operating storefront as proof of occupancy permit status.
Do not use aggregate PSA construction data as named-building evidence.
manual_follow_up_triggers: user needs a certified true copy, official confirmation, or legal-grade permit finding
only process pages exist for the target LGU
portal requires application, owner, permit, or tracking identifiers
records involve owner, lessee, applicant, or authorized representative status
public evidence conflicts with user-provided records or building status

primary_source_types: LGU, OBO, OCBO, CDRRMO, or City Engineer incident posts
DPWH, PIA, PNA, or national agency situation reports
reputable Philippine news reports
official social media posts as leads when no durable page exists
manual LGU or engineering-office inspection report requests

example_sources.name: Davao City red/yellow tag announcement
example_sources.access_notes: Official OCBO/LGU page naming red and yellow tags, damage language, and clearance-before-reoccupancy requirements.

example_sources.name: Davao OCBO tagged buildings safety requirements
example_sources.access_notes: Official page explaining tag effects, comprehensive reports, repair methodology, and permit review requirements.

example_sources.name: Davao City structural assessments continue
example_sources.access_notes: Official assessment-process page and public crack-report routing context.

example_sources.name: Davao City Engineer unsafe occupancy list
example_sources.access_notes: Official page identifying unsafe public buildings after an earthquake and repair funding path.

example_sources.name: PIA DPWH faster road repairs after quake
example_sources.access_notes: Government news example for assessment, clearing, and infrastructure damage context.

example_sources.name: DSWD DROMIC earthquake incident reports
example_sources.access_notes: Useful for disaster context and damaged-house/displacement reporting, usually not named-building tag proof.

example_sources.name: MindaNews Davao red/yellow tag map
example_sources.access_notes: News mapping of tagged structures; should be reconciled to OCBO or LGU sources when possible.

example_sources.name: GMA Regional TV unsafe buildings report
example_sources.access_notes: News example confirming tag meanings, repair, and OCBO clearance language.

example_sources.name: Inquirer Mindanao's tallest building yellow tag report
example_sources.access_notes: Building-specific news example with restricted use until repair and fit-for-reoccupancy declaration.

required_user_inputs: confirmed exact building, branch, wing, tower, or complex
city or LGU jurisdiction and address or barangay
incident type such as earthquake, fire, collapse, closure, repair, or retrofit
incident date, event name, or year range when known
known aliases, tenant names, tower names, school names, or mall wing names
target assertion: damage history, closure, reopening, repair, retrofit, unsafe tag, or clearance
expected_public_result_shape: dated incident card with source URL, issuing or reporting body, and evidence strength
tag, closure, restricted-use, reopening, repair, retrofit, or assessment language when publicly reported
official-source priority ranking over reputable news, official social media, and private social posts
manual_request_needed when reports reference OCBO, OBO, City Engineer, or inspection documents not posted online
not_assessable_from_public_web when no public source can support the requested incident claim
limitations: Detailed structural assessment reports and repair methodologies are often not posted publicly.
Some official lists appear only on social media and may be less durable than LGU pages.
Search works best with a named building; address-only incident lookup is weaker.
Absence of a public incident record does not prove no incident, damage, closure, repair, or retrofit occurred.
Reopening or clearance is often harder to prove publicly than initial closure or tag issuance.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim the building is currently safe from old clearance, old reopening, or old news reports.
Do not claim the building is unsafe unless a current official source or clearly dated report directly says so.
Do not treat private social videos, photos, or comments as proof of structural damage.
Do not infer complete incident history from public search results.
Do not convert local red/yellow tag language into a nationwide uniform rating without local-source context.
manual_follow_up_triggers: official source says inspection or assessment is ongoing
only news or social evidence exists for a named building
red or yellow tag exists but no lifting, reopening, or clearance source is public
source references OCBO, OBO, City Engineer, or inspection reports that are not attached online
target is high-occupancy use such as mall, hotel, school, hospital, condo, BPO, or transport terminal
user needs a compliance-grade or engineering conclusion rather than public-evidence screening

primary_source_types: PCAB contractor license verification
PRC professional license verification
SEC and DTI company or business-name lookup routes
DHSUD license-to-sell and project/developer lists
PhilGEPS and DPWH awarded-contract pages
developer, operator, project, and public procurement pages

example_sources.name: CIAP listing for PCAB online license verification
example_sources.access_notes: Official route to PCAB contractor license verification.

example_sources.name: PCAB contractor license verification
example_sources.access_notes: Can verify a named contractor or license record; cannot identify an unnamed contractor from an address alone.

example_sources.name: PRC license verification
example_sources.access_notes: Can verify a named professional when identifiers are sufficient; does not prove involvement in a building.

example_sources.name: SEC Express System
example_sources.access_notes: Company documents may involve paid or manual request workflows; identity evidence only.

example_sources.name: SEC API Marketplace
example_sources.access_notes: Public route for company information access, subject to account/API/payment limits.

example_sources.name: DTI Business Name Search
example_sources.access_notes: Can support business-name identity checks, not building compliance or construction involvement.

example_sources.name: DHSUD list of license to sell
example_sources.access_notes: Can support developer/project evidence for covered real-estate sales, not general building-code compliance.

example_sources.name: DPWH awarded civil works contracts
example_sources.access_notes: Can link named public works projects and contractors when the target appears in procurement records.

required_user_inputs: confirmed exact place and jurisdiction
suspected contractor, architect, engineer, developer, operator, or company name
profession, license number, PCAB license number, SEC registration number, or DTI business name if available
project name, DHSUD license-to-sell project name, or procurement reference if available
source lead such as permit photo, project billboard, developer page, procurement award, press release, or plaque
expected_public_result_shape: named-party verification result with source URL, match confidence, and caveats
company or business-name identity evidence when public or manually requestable
project-specific linkage only when a source connects the party to the target building or project
name_required when no contractor, professional, developer, or operator name is known
not_publicly_provable when public records cannot link a named party to the exact building
limitations: Address-only searches are usually insufficient for contractor or professional verification.
PCAB, PRC, SEC, DTI, and DHSUD records verify identities or statuses; they do not prove building-specific compliance.
A contractor or professional license check does not prove that person or firm worked on the target building.
Developer marketing pages are self-published and need corroboration for regulated claims.
SEC and DTI records may identify entities but not current owner, operator, or permit responsibility.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim a contractor, engineer, architect, developer, or operator worked on the building unless a project-specific source links them.
Do not claim the building is compliant or safe from PCAB, PRC, SEC, DTI, DHSUD, or developer-page evidence.
Do not claim a party is unlicensed from one failed search or spelling mismatch.
Do not treat developer marketing as government approval.
Do not claim current owner or operator solely from an SEC or DTI identity record.
manual_follow_up_triggers: the user has no named contractor, professional, developer, or operator
public sources conflict on a party name or role
the target is private and not tied to public procurement or developer project pages
license search requires personal identifiers or exact spelling not available publicly
user needs legally reliable attribution, signed plans, permit attachments, or professional-of-record confirmation

primary_source_types: PD 1096 National Building Code text and IRR references
DPWH and NBCDO reference pages and circulars
Philippine Green Building Code references
NSCP and ASEP publication context
Fire Code, accessibility, and referral-code context
LGU process pages when standards point to local implementation

example_sources.name: Supreme Court E-Library PD 1096
example_sources.access_notes: Public legal reference for the National Building Code.

example_sources.name: DPWH PD 1096 reference page
example_sources.access_notes: DPWH page for PD 1096 and related references.

example_sources.name: DPWH National Building Code references
example_sources.access_notes: DPWH NBC reference landing page.

example_sources.name: Philippine Green Building Code booklet
example_sources.access_notes: Public DPWH PDF for Philippine Green Building Code context.

example_sources.name: NBCDO Memorandum Circular 01 s. 2016
example_sources.access_notes: NBCDO circular related to Green Building Code implementation context.

example_sources.name: ASEP
example_sources.access_notes: ASEP public site for structural engineering publication and professional context.

example_sources.name: RA 9514 Fire Code
example_sources.access_notes: Public legal reference for Fire Code context.

example_sources.name: NCDA revised 2024 BP 344 IRR
example_sources.access_notes: Accessibility referral context; not proof of a building's accessibility compliance.

required_user_inputs: confirmed place if the user wants context framed around a particular building type
general question or standard category such as National Building Code, Fire Code, accessibility, green building, or structural code
building use type or occupancy class when the user wants standards framed generally
whether the user wants general standards context or building-specific evidence
gross floor area, project type, or new-versus-alteration context when screening Green Building Code applicability at a high level
expected_public_result_shape: standards-context summary with citations to laws, circulars, code references, or official process pages
separate note that context does not prove the target building complied
manual_request_needed when full plans, calculations, inspections, certificates, or professional review are needed
not_publicly_provable for building-specific compliance without official records or professional review
handoff to permit, incident, or professional scope when the user asks a building-specific question
limitations: Standards context cannot verify plan review, construction quality, inspections, or certificates for a named building.
Full professional standards or code texts may not be available as clean official free web documents.
Building-specific compliance requires signed and sealed plans, calculations, inspections, permits, certificates, or professional review.
LGU and BFP implementation details vary, so national context may not fully answer local process questions.
General standards cannot establish current safety or fitness for occupancy.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim a named building is code-compliant from standards text alone.
Do not claim a named building is structurally safe, earthquake-safe, fire-safe, or fit for occupancy from standards context.
Do not claim a valid permit, occupancy permit, FSEC, or FSIC exists unless an official record directly supports it.
Do not claim NSCP design compliance without signed and sealed plans, calculations, review, or authoritative record.
Do not present standards context as legal or engineering certification.
manual_follow_up_triggers: user asks whether the specific building is compliant, safe, or fit for occupancy
user needs plan, calculation, inspection, FSEC, FSIC, or occupancy certificate verification
question depends on local OBO, BFP, zoning, accessibility, or other implementation records
source text is not publicly available or is only summarized by a secondary source
the target is high occupancy or the claim could affect safety decisions

primary_source_types: standards context
LGU OBO and permit-process routes
BFP, FSEC, FSIC, and fire-safety process routes
contractor, professional, developer, and operator lookup routes
incident, damage, closure, repair, and retrofit public evidence
FOI and manual request paths
citation and overclaim review

example_sources.name: DPWH National Building Code references
example_sources.access_notes: Baseline standards context for the packet; not building-specific proof.

example_sources.name: Quezon City Department of the Building Official
example_sources.access_notes: Example LGU OBO route for permit/occupancy process and manual follow-up.

example_sources.name: Davao City Office of the City Building Official
example_sources.access_notes: Example LGU OCBO route for permits, occupancy, annual inspections, certifications, and complaints.

example_sources.name: BFP eFOI agency page
example_sources.access_notes: BFP FOI route for fire-safety records and process questions; requests may be redirected to local fire stations.

example_sources.name: BFP FSIC request example
example_sources.access_notes: Example showing FSIC requests may require local station routing and cannot be treated as a central public lookup.

example_sources.name: PCAB contractor license verification
example_sources.access_notes: Use only when a contractor name or license identifier is available.

example_sources.name: PRC license verification
example_sources.access_notes: Use only when a professional name or identifier is available.

example_sources.name: Davao City red/yellow tag announcement
example_sources.access_notes: Official incident-history example for tags, damage language, and reoccupancy caveats.

example_sources.name: FOI FAQ
example_sources.access_notes: Process context for FOI limitations, request paths, and requester obligations.

required_user_inputs: confirmed exact place packet from Gate 1
locked broad public-evidence scope from Gate 2
city or LGU jurisdiction, address, barangay, and any branch or wing detail
known owner, operator, developer, contractor, professional, permit, FSIC, FSEC, or application identifiers if available
time window for construction, occupancy, incident, repair, retrofit, or closure questions
whether the user wants public-web screening only or a manual follow-up checklist
expected_public_result_shape: multi-lane evidence packet with source URLs, evidence strength, source type, automation status, and caveats
standards context separated from building-specific evidence
manual_request_needed items listed as follow-up tasks, not treated as completed searches
not_found and not_assessable_from_public_web labels for gaps that public search cannot resolve
citation review that removes or weakens any unsupported compliance, safety, risk, or legal-status language
limitations: A broad packet can collect public evidence and gaps, but it still cannot certify compliance, safety, risk, or fitness for occupancy.
Some lanes require names, permit numbers, dates, or authorized-party details before they can be meaningfully searched.
Manual, FOI, paid, or local-office records must be represented as request paths unless the user explicitly authorizes a request.
Public search can be biased toward major cities, large incidents, prominent developers, and well-indexed LGUs.
Evidence from different lanes may refer to different entities: tenant, operator, developer, building owner, contractor, complex, wing, or tower.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim code compliance, noncompliance, structural safety, earthquake safety, fire safety, or fitness for occupancy from a broad public packet.
Do not claim a place has no permit, no incident, no contractor, or no fire certificate from missing public results.
Do not collapse tenant, building, complex, owner, operator, developer, and contractor identities into one entity.
Do not publish accusations, personal data, or legal conclusions based on partial public evidence.
Do not perform FOI submissions, paid document requests, credentialed portal searches, agency contacts, or third-party writes without explicit approval.
manual_follow_up_triggers: any lane returns only process pages, incomplete identifiers, or conflicting public evidence
the user asks for legal, engineering, insurance, financing, leasing, or safety-critical reliance
a public source points to an unpublished inspection, permit, certificate, repair methodology, or professional report
the target is a high-occupancy or high-consequence facility
the evidence packet would otherwise need to say compliant, noncompliant, safe, unsafe, fit for occupancy, or no permit

ecc_review.objective: Gate 2 locks the public-evidence scope and maps it to source realities; it does not run building-specific research.
inputs: user's scope choice or natural-language scope request
outputs: blocked evidence lanes until scope confirmation
eval: all five Gate 2 scopes are present
each scope declares at least one source type, limitation, forbidden claim, and automation status
example source URLs are valid HTTP or HTTPS URLs
no source-reality record claims building-specific evidence was searched during Gate 2
ecc_review.handoff: Gate 3 may use the locked scope and this matrix to define the public-evidence packet schema.
human_boundary: user must explicitly confirm the exact place before Gate 2 starts
user must explicitly confirm the audit scope before evidence search starts
manual or FOI requests, paid records, credentialed portals, uploads, and agency contacts require explicit approval
safety_risks: turning missing public evidence into a negative compliance claim
treating standards context as building-specific proof
overstating old incident reports as current safety findings
exposing personal or sensitive requester data through FOI or manual forms
mixing tenant, building, complex, developer, operator, and contractor identities
ecc_review.overclaim_boundary: The auditor may report public evidence and gaps, but must not certify legal compliance, structural safety, earthquake safety, fire safety, or fitness for occupancy.
